LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF DAVID LAMPACH'S MOTION FOR COURT APPROVAL OF SETTLEMENT OF SHAREHOLDER DERIVATIVE CLAIM IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS FAIR AND REASONABLE TO THE CORPORATION AND ITS SHAREHOLDERS AND NOT THE PRODUCT OF FRAUD, OVERREACHING OR COLLUSION BY THE SETTLING PARTIES AND IS OTHERWISE WITHIN THE RANGE OF SETTLEMENTS OF DERIVATIVE CLAIMS THAT WARRANT COURT APPROVAL. COUNSEL FOR MR. LAMPACH IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. PLAINTIFF DAVID LAMPACH's Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement Of Shareholder Derivative Case.

Plaintiff David Lampach's motion for court approval of settlement of shareholder derivative claim is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The court finds that the settlement is fair and reasonable to the corporation and its shareholders and not the product of fraud, overreaching or collusion by the settling parties and is otherwise within the range of settlements of derivative claims that warrant court approval.

Counsel for Mr. Lampach is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the ruling.

Off Calendar Jan-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Scher, Esq. of Henry G. Wykowski & Associates, T:415.788.4545, for defendans/moving party;
Jason R. Bartlett, Esq. of Mauriel Kapouytian Woods LLP, T:415.738.6334, for defendant Steep Hill;
Gerald P. Burleson, Esq. of the Law Offices of Gerald P. Burleson, T:858.794.4805, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JMICHAELE KELLER and DELFT BLUE HORIZONS, B.V.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Fifth Cause Of Action For Securities Fraud, In Third Amended Complaint, filed by plaintiff David Lampach is GRANTED. By presenting Mr. Lampach's responses to the deemed-served discovery, Mr. Keller and Delft satisfied their summary adjudication burden that Mr. Lampach has no information that either of the defendants made any communication in connection with the offer for the sale or purchase of securities in the consulting agreement that either included an untrue statement of material fact or omitted a material fact that was needed to avoid a misleading statement. Mr. Lampach has not presented any evidence creating a triable dispute on this issue.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF DAVID LAMPACH's Motion To File Third Amended Complaint as follows:

Plaintiff David Lampach's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is granted on the following conditions that must be accepted in writing or orally on the record by Mr. Lampach's counsel at or prior to the hearing: a) No later than October 25, 2017 Mr. Lampach will provide code-compliant verified responses without objections other than privilege to the deemed-served discovery requests that he state all information that supports his new fifth cause of action, identify and provide full contact information for all persons who know such information and state what information each of them knows, and identify and produce all documents that reflect or relate to all such information and b) Mr. Lampach agrees that defendants Delft Blue Horizons B.V. and JMichaele Keller can bring a summary motion directed to the fifth cause of action on thirty, rather than seventy-five, days' notice and that the motion can be heard on or before January 8, 2018. Granting the motion is in accord with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions imposed on granting the motion eliminates any prejudice to the Delft Blue and Mr. Keller.

[After hearing, the Court signed the proposed order submitted by email in accordance with the tentative ruling.]

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jerry Burleson of the law offices of Gerald P. Burleson (858-794-4805) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jason R. Bartlett of the law offices of Mauriel Kapouytian Woods LLP (415-738-6334) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Steep Hill Labs, Inc.

Attorney, Andrew Scher of the law offices of Henry G. Wykowski & Associate (415-788-4545) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Steep Hill Labs, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT STEEP HILL LABS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Defendant Steep Hill Labs, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Steep Hill Labs failed to satisfy its burden to show that the members of the SLC (Stephen DeAngelo and Addision De Moura) are independent from defendants Jmichaele Keller and Delft Blue Horizons and, even if Steep Hill Labs did satisfy its burden, plaintiff David Lampach presented sufficient facts creating a triable dispute. Steep Hill Labs's argument that the cessation of any financial or business relationship outside of Steep Hill Labs between the members of the SLC and Mr. Keller and his company establishes the required independence lacks merit. Steep Hills Labs has not cited any authority for this argument and common sense indicates that mere cessation of an otherwise consequential relationship does not, without more, shows that the parties to the relationship will not be swayed in their decision-making either by their prior relationship or the hope for a future relationship. Based on the recency of the cessation of the relationships of the members of the SLC with Mr. Keller and Delft Blue as well as the amount of money involved in those relationships, a reasonable trier of fact could draw the reasonable inference that the members of the SLC in performing the work of the SLC were "governed by extraneous considerations or influences" rather than the "merits of the issue[s]" addressed by the SLC. (Desaigoudar v. Meyercord (2003) 108 Cal. App. 4th at 189, quoting Katz v. Chevron Corp. (1994) 22 Cal. App. 4th 1352, 1367, in turn quoting Kaplan v. Wyatt (Del. Super. Ct. 1985) 499 A. 2d 1184, 1189). The facts presented by Mr. Lambach - which tend to show that in developing and documenting their relationships outside Steep Hill Labs the members of the SLC and Mr. Keller and Delft Blue committed themselves to positions favorable to Mr. Keller and Delft Blue vis-a-vis Steep Hill Labs and antagonistic toward the positions of Mr. Lambach -- make the drawing of an inference of lack of independence by the members of the SLC even more likely. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312 and C.C.P. 437(c)(g).

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, e-mail: geales@sftc.org. (L&M302)
